understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to use the limited step budget to maneuver the controllable/selectable pieces around the grid, interacting with obstacles (walls) and pushable blocks as needed, likely to reach or align with goal tiles/positions (shown as goals in the object list/semantic grid).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each MOVE action attempted to shift the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction, updating position by ±3 on x or y when successful; if blocked by a wall/boundary or an immovable object, the piece stayed in place but still consumed a step. Some adjacent objects could be pushable, in which case moving into them would try to push them along in the same direction (and fail if the pushed object was blocked). Levels could contain multiple selectable pieces; using CLICK with a target_position (in the same pixel coordinates as player.position/objects) switched control to the selectable at that exact position, after which subsequent MOVE commands affected that newly selected piece.
